speaker: interviewer
text: [INAUDIBLE] This is roll number 5.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mr.-- [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] Would you like to introduce this lovely lady next to you?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, I will be happy. This is my beautiful wife, the mother of our two beautiful daughters. Her name is Nuna, and in English, her name is Ann. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] She went through her life not less than I went through. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And thank God she came out. And maybe she will be able to tell the world, not just the Jewish, the whole world what she went through. Maybe this will help to make a better world. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS] Can you tell us who is this person here in the picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] my father. It so happen my-- my uncle was here in the United State, who my father helped a lot to go to America. And I find the picture-- the picture in-- in my aunt's house, and she made a copy of the picture, and she gave it to me. When it was sent? When it was, I have no idea.

speaker: interviewer
text: What's his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Whose name?

speaker: interviewer
text: Your father's name.

speaker: interviewee
text: My father's name is Haim. He was the only son. My grandfather was-- he was born in Dubienka. This is a town in Poland.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, His name was Haim?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. His name was Haim.

speaker: interviewer
text: Gold?

speaker: interviewee
text: Goldbarten, and he was the only son.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK thank you. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my mother. She was born in Komarow. My-- my-- her-- her father's name was Levy Yitzjack. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And her-- her maiden name was Bina Silberstein. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And like I said, she was-- she was born in Komorow. When she was born, I cannot say when. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] This picture is of me and my wife. My name is Emil Goldbarten. My wife's name is Ann Goldbarten. This was taken in a DP Camp. The date, I'm sorry I cannot-- I don't remember, and it so happened I forgot to put on the other side the name.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture is of my wife and my two daughters. It was a taken in DP camp in Eschwege. It's-- it's my older daughter, Haya, or in English, Claire, and my younger daughter, Bina. It's still in English, Bina.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, this picture is, from the right, is my best friend, and this is the father of my older daughter, who-- who was killed by the Germans.

speaker: interviewer
text: What's his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: His name was Josef Langer. He was my next door neighbor, my best friend. Next to him, on the left-hand side, is my older sister, Miriam. She also at the same time perished, and when they killed the whole town.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: Here we are. This picture was taken probably in the DP Camp in Eschwege. Here is my whole family. It's me, Emil Goldbarten. Sitting next to me is my wife, Ann Goldbarten. To the left standing is my older daughter, [INAUDIBLE] Claire Goldbarten. And my wife is holding my younger daughter, who was born in Eschwege in the DP Camp, Bina.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Be a good girl. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my wife's first cousin, [? Morris ?] Rosenberg. He's the one who sponsored us, and he brought us to the United States.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was the picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: I cannot-- I cannot remember.

speaker: interviewer
text: You remember the country?

speaker: interviewee
text: Here, he took-- [INAUDIBLE] It was taken in the United States.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture is me when I came to the United States. I changed. I don't look like that no more.

speaker: interviewer
text: Which year?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was-- we came to United States in September or the beginning of October 1949.

speaker: interviewer
text: And your name?

speaker: interviewee
text: My name is Emil Goldbarten.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken in the United State. The date I don't remember. This picture is my whole family, my daughters, my son-in-laws, and my grandchildren. I think I have a beautiful family.

speaker: interviewer
text: You do. What are their names from right to left?

speaker: interviewee
text: From right to left is my oldest grandson, Jonathan Borren. And then comes me, Emil Goldbarten. And then comes my older son-in-law Adam Borren. And this is my older daughter, Claire, now-- Claire Goldbarten, now she is Claire Borren. Next to Claire is my youngest son-in-law Ira Addes. And next to Ira Addes is my older granddaughter, Sarah Borren And next to Sarah Borren is my younger daughter, Bina Addes. Bina Addes. Bina Goldbarten Addes. And next to Bina is my beautiful wife Ann Goldbarten.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken last year. It was the bat mitzvah of my granddaughter, [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] [? Danyel, ?] taken in Amherst, Mass. The new beauty-- here is my granddaughter sitting between my younger daughter here and between my wife, this is [? Danyel ?] Addes. This was her bat mitzvah. And also there are two kids from my son-in-law's brother, those are the other two girls on the bottom. My son-in-law's brother with his wife, they are from the left on the top standing from the left.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the other ones are all your family? [INAUDIBLE]

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, I mean-- and yeah, the rest is all my family, except this is my grandson from the younger daughter here. His name is Ari. And his real name is after my father, Haim, just my son-in-law gave him the name from the Exodus, whatever there was, the famous name Ari.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, thank you very much.
